title: gaultheria minima
intro: Gaultheria minima, also known as New Zealand teaberry or small mountain pepper, is a type of flowering plant in the heath family that is native to the mountains of New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of salicylic acid and other compounds. Gaultheria minima has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent.
